steps.title: Spot the logical gap
steps.content: Native plants depend on fruit-eating birds for seed dispersal. All native fruit-eating birds have gone extinct. Yet introduced birds are now spreading seeds from these native plants. This shows that introduced birds have taken on a role the extinct native birds can no longer perform.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice A directly addresses this gap: non-native birds play a role that native birds used to fill. The others speculate about extinction risk or population growth without textual support, or make unsupported comparative claims about seed quantities. That matches choice A — A is correct.
